title: Melkor conversion loss report v1
description: The complete, machine-readable account of what a format conversion lost. Every committed conversion emits one, including a zero-loss report, so automation never has to infer whether reporting was omitted. Losses are distinct from validation diagnostics: a malformed input or a resource-limit failure is an error, not a loss.

items.description: Every loss the conversion incurred. Empty for a lossless conversion.

code.description: A stable loss code. Its meaning never changes across the 2.x line.

severity.description: info/warning are recorded and pass; severe blocks unless the exact code is approved; fatal always blocks and cannot be approved.

approved_codes.description: The exact loss codes the caller approved (e.g. via --allow-loss). Recorded in the committed report so a downstream reviewer can see which losses were deliberately accepted.
